caribbean.headline: Barbados Sees Heavy Rain, 6.6 ft Seas
caribbean.paragraph: Barbados is experiencing east-southeast swell with sustained winds near 16 kt, seas up to 6.6 ft and a 7 s dominant swell period, accompanied by heavy tropical showers with 100% rain probability. I am monitoring conditions across the Lesser Antilles; St. Lucia reports lighter winds near 10 kt, and while winds and seas remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, mariners should expect brief heavy downpours and reduced visibility.

mediterranean.headline: Rain Focused on Naples with Light Winds and Small Seas
mediterranean.paragraph: Naples is experiencing southeasterly winds near 13 kt with 1.9 ft seas and a WSW-dominant 5 s swell, and the forecast shows 62% rain probability today. I, James Van Fleet, note Cadiz as the runner-up with winds up to 20 kt and wave heights similar to Naples around 1.9 ft, so I recommend mariners prepare for wet conditions and brief heavy showers rather than significant sea state changes.
